source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/07_Retrofit (HnX-R1)/★ FDA 510(k) - K252912/참고자료/참고 - 2,3G FDA 문서/VOL_011_Appendix J Clinical Test Report/002_Clinical Consideration - A1417MCW.docx

sourceSummary.oneLine: Clinical comparison report for A1417MCW against HAD1417MCW using plain radiographic images across 30 anatomical views.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports clinical image quality comparison for a device submission package referenced under FDA 510(k) source materials.
keyPoints: Objective Device is A1417MCW by H&abyz and Comparative Equipment is HAD1417MCW made by H&Abyz.
Output data for communications standard DICOM 3.0 is used for downloading and analysis.
Thirty radiographic views were evaluated with grade scale S, A, B, C.
Total Average was 3.62 for HAD1417MCW and 3.63 for A1417MCW.
The conclusion states A1417MCW images are comparable or superior to the same view obtained from HAD1417MCW.

concepts.name: Clinical image quality equivalence
concepts.evidence: the images obtained with the A1417MCW are comparable or superior to the same view obtained from a similar patient with the HAD1417MCW

evidence.evidenceText: Approval Quality Manager Hur Namkyu Dec/22/2022

evidence.claim: The report uses DICOM 3.0 for output data formatting for downloading and analysis.
evidence.evidenceText: output data for communications standard DICOM 3.0 is used to format at the data for downloading and analysis

evidence.claim: The clinical image review evaluated 30 anatomical radiographic views.
evidence.evidenceText: Average Value of each annotation No Contents HAD1417MCW A1417MCW 1 Skull PA ... 30 Knee Lat

evidence.claim: The total average evaluation score was 3.62 for HAD1417MCW and 3.63 for A1417MCW.
The report concludes A1417MCW images are comparable or superior to HAD1417MCW images.
evidence.evidenceText: the images obtained with the A1417MCW are comparable or superior to the same view obtained from a similar patient with the HAD1417MCW

evidence.claim: The report states there is no difficulty evaluating anatomical structures necessary to provide a correct conclusion.
evidence.evidenceText: There is no difficulty in evaluating a wide range of anatomic structures necessary to provide a correct conclusion.

evidence.claim: The report states soft tissues on extremity films were seen with better clarity using A1417MCW.
evidence.evidenceText: Specifically, the soft tissues on extremity films were seen with better clarity.

reviewFlags.reason: Authority FDA is indicated by source metadata and sourcePath, but the document text itself does not state FDA authority or jurisdiction.
No revision or version number is present in the provided source text.
Knee AP section lists F1417MCW in one comparison table although the document otherwise identifies A1417MCW as the objective device.
Manufacturer name appears with inconsistent capitalization: H&abyz and H&Abyz.
OCR/text quality issues appear in terms such as DCIOM, Sternal Angel, Insperction, and unremarkable spacing.
